contract.status: authored semantic vectors; stack harnesses pending; not ratified

conventions.identityRule: Channel correlation identities are opaque and never equal host-native Execution or Occurrence identities.
conventions.harnessBoundary: Each stack supplies its own adapter; this file contains data and expected observations only.

given: A valid request carries channel c1, request r1, and optional execution e1
when: The peer returns a terminal successful Outcome echoing every carried identity
then: The host records a distinct host-native Execution identity

given: A valid request carries request r1
when: The claimed Outcome carries request r2

given: The endpoint recognizes contract version 1
when: A frame declares version 2

given: Compatibility is negotiated or fixed before invocation
when: The peer names an unrecognized contract or required feature

given: A structurally valid envelope
when: Its kind or body discriminator is unknown

given: A complete frame
when: Required envelope fields are absent or malformed

given: A request input uses additive open payload Shape version 2
The consumer recognizes version 1
when: The consumer projects the payload to version 1
then: The request remains eligible for authority evaluation
Unknown optional payload structure is ignored canonically

given: An authority Constraint value version 2 adds narrowing semantics
The evaluator recognizes only version 1
when: The request presents that authority position
then: The atom is Unknown and the standalone request is denied
No frame is sent across a pre-provider boundary

given: Authority is AnyOf(SatisfiedKnown, Unknown)
when: The target evaluates the request structurally

given: Authority is AllOf(SatisfiedKnown, Unknown)
when: The target evaluates the request structurally
then: The request is denied without a far-side effect

given: A Channel crosses a declared trust boundary
when: A request body contains a Capability or omits no-capability-transfer

given: The local receiving authority boundary denies a request
when: The host records the attempted Execution
then: No Channel denial frame is emitted

given: An authorized request reaches the provider
when: The domain Operation reports structured failure details
then: The failure is not a protocol or process failure

given: Two realizations use different local code strings for an unsupported contract
when: Each adapter reports the portable observation

given: Replay protection and its window are declared
Request r1 was accepted inside the window
when: Request r1 is received again inside the window

given: A realization declares a finite frame or parse bound
when: An inbound frame exceeds that bound

given: A request was sent and no valid Outcome was received
when: The peer process terminates
then: The observation is peer-terminated in remote-provider or unknown failure domain
Retry and fallback facts are explicit

given: A semantic Outcome or protocol-error frame
when: It contains an exception, stack trace, runtime type, or authority object representation
then: The frame is rejected before semantic use
The category is malformed-message or invalid-payload according to the declared field position

given: A compatible established contract
when: A request names an Operation outside that contract

given: A recognized request Operation and payload contract
when: The input cannot conform through permitted payload projection

given: A realization declares a lifecycle state machine
when: A recognized message kind is illegal in the current state

given: The endpoint cannot continue protocol processing
when: Its local implementation raises a runtime-specific failure
then: No exception, stack trace, or runtime type crosses

given: No valid semantic Outcome is available
when: The independent stack adapter classifies the local observation
then: It selects exactly one declared process category
Unknown retains the reason narrower attribution was impossible

given: Equivalent failures are observed at different Channel boundaries
when: Each adapter records the failure relative to its observer
then: It selects exactly one declared failure domain
No domain value claims global topology
